title: Darth Malak
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]]|the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] [[Malak (Rodian)|Malak]]}} {{Otheruses|Alek}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Malakart-crop.jpg]] |name=Darth Malak
Alek |homeworld=[[Quelii]][[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]] |birth=Quelii{{Topps|set=2012 Topps Star Wars Galactic Files Series 1|cardname=Darth Malak (Sith Lord)|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/73989/cid/5329817/2012-Topps-Star-Wars:-Galactic-Files-186-Darth-Malak}} |death=[[3956 BBY]], [[Star Forge]], [[Lehon system]]The ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', including the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Malak on the [[Star Forge]] in the [[Lehon system]], to [[3956 BBY]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{DB|character|darthmalak|Malak, Darth}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |height=2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]][[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]] |feathers= |eyes=*Blue[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] *Gray {{C|[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]}} |skin=Fair |cyber=[[Cybernetics/Legends|Prosthetic jaw]] |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]][[Knights of the Old Republic 31|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 31]] **[[Revanchist]]s *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Reconstituted Sith Empire]] {{C|briefly}}{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War|int=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War}} *[[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]] |masters=*[[Zhar Lestin]] {{C|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]}} *[[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] {{C|[[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]]}} |apprentices=*[[Darth Bandon]] {{C|[[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]}} *[[Bastila Shan]] {{C|Sith apprentice}} }} {{Quote|There's nobody left who remembers who I was before. But they'll remember Malak. I'll make sure of that.|Malak|[[Knights of the Old Republic 42|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 42]]}} '''Darth Malak''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who reigned as a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Before becoming a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], '''Alek''' was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Quelii]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. When the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] [[Battle of Quelii|attacked]] his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], he escaped the battle and fled to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], whose immigration records assigned the name of his [[Squinquargesimus|home village]], '''Squinquargesimus,''' as his surname. He was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and was nicknamed '''Squint''' by his fellow [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], before eventually becoming a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. During the [[Mandalorian Wars]], Alek, [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]—his closest friend and fellow Jedi Knight—and their [[Revanchist|faction of Jedi]] disobeyed the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] and aided the Republic's war effort through [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] missions in the Outer Rim. He befriended the Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] and, after taking the name "'''Malak'''" in order to avoid an arrest warrant issued by the Council, assisted Carrick in [[Vindication|defeating]] the secretive [[Jedi Covenant]]. As the war progressed, Malak became a [[Republic Military/Legends|military]] [[Jedi General/Legends|general]] and gained a reputation as a headstrong warrior who recklessly charged into danger. Malak and Revan ultimately defeated the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorians]] in the [[Battle of Malachor V|war's final battle]] at the planet [[Malachor V]] and pursued the surviving Mandalorians into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. However, the two Jedi encountered the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted remnants]] of the ancient [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] and fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] under his tutelage, becoming Sith Lords. The newly christened [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Malak and Darth Revan were given a mission by the [[Sith Emperor/Legends|Sith Emperor]] to find the [[Star Forge]], an ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]], which would help to destroy the Jedi Order and the Republic. Malak helped Revan track down the [[Star Map]]s that led to the Star Forge and, after finding the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] above the world [[Lehon/Legends|Rakata Prime]], Revan assumed the mantle of Dark Lord of the Sith, with Malak becoming his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. [[3959 BBY|A year after]] their disappearance from known Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]], the two founded their own [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], returned to the Republic, and declared war, determined to end the Jedi Order's supposed tyranny. At [[Duel on Revan's flagship|one point]] during the conflict that became known as the Jedi Civil War, Malak's entire jaw was removed by a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] strike from Darth Revan, forcing him to wear a large metal [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthesis]]. A short [[Time/Legends|time]] later, after the Jedi dispatched a strike team to [[Capture of Darth Revan|capture]] Revan and Malak, the latter betrayed his [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], ordering the guns of his [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|flagship]] to fire on [[Darth Revan's flagship|Revan's vessel]]. Convinced that his Master had [[Death/Legends|died]], Malak usurped Revan's mantle of Dark Lord and ruled the Empire unchallenged, brutally conquering territory after territory and taking the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|fallen Jedi]] [[Darth Bandon]] as his [[Shadow Hand]]. It was not long until Malak was proven wrong about his beliefs regarding Revan's death. The [[3956 BBY|following year]], after [[Destruction of Taris|destroying]] the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|razing]] the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the planet [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], Malak [[Skirmish aboard the Leviathan|intercepted]] Jedi Knight [[Bastila Shan]] and an amnesiac Revan, whose memories as the Dark Lord had been replaced with an identity loyal to the Republic by the Jedi Council. After Malak revealed to Revan his former identity as the Dark Lord, Shan allowed herself to be captured by Malak so that Revan could escape. Malak corrupted Shan, whom he had once considered a threat, and convinced her to become his apprentice, replacing Darth Bandon, whom Revan and his companions had [[Mission to Hrakert Station|previously killed]]. Now assisted by Shan's [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], Malak considered his Sith Empire to be invincible. However, when the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Battle of Rakata Prime|attacked]] the Star Forge, Revan—a [[Light side of the Force/Legends|redeemed]] and retrained Jedi—boarded the ancient superweapon and confronted Malak in a lightsaber duel that resulted in the Dark Lord's death. With that, Malak's reign as the Dark Lord ended, and the Republic eventually emerged victorious from the Jedi Civil War. ==Biography== ===Early life and Jedi training=== {{Quote|The Jedi Order moved too slowly for Revan and Malak; we were too cautious in their eyes. They always sought to learn far quicker than their Masters felt was prudent.|Zhar Lestin|audio=ZharRevanandMalak.flac|}} [[File:YoungAlek.png|thumb|left|200px|A young Alek]] In [[3993 BBY]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]]—in the presence of the fellow Jedi [[Shaela Nuur]] within a [[Dreshdae]] settlement abode on the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith tombworld]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]—had a future [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Malak and a [[Jedi Civil War|looming conflict]] featuring the [[Destruction of Taris|devastation]] of an [[Taris/Legends|ecumenopolis]], the [[Skirmish aboard the Leviathan|capture]] of a [[Bastila Shan|Jedi]], and the [[Slavery/Legends|enslavement]] of [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]]. Qel-Droma soon had another vision of the future as{{Tales|23|Shadows and Light}} he{{CSWECite|III|55|Qei-Droma, Duron}} lay [[Death/Legends|dying]], in which he foresaw the [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeat]] of Malak at the hands of the Jedi [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was later known as Darth Malak was born Alek on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Quelii]] in a village named [[Squinquargesimus]]. After Alek escaped the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]' [[Battle of Quelii|devastation]] of his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]],{{DHBoards|url=Help/Boards/viewtopic.php?t=16225|thread=Question for John Jackson Miller|nobackup=1}} he fled to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]; the name of his home village was assigned as his surname by the Republic's immigration records. Eventually, Alek was found to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] as a Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] at the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the vegetative planet [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] During his [[Time/Legends|time]] as a Padawan, Alek was nicknamed "Squint" by his peers,[[Knights of the Old Republic 10|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 10]] because they had difficulty pronouncing his surname.{{CSWECite|III|185|Squint}} Alek befriended a fellow Padawan who would eventually be known as "Revan."''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' Though Revan was recognized as the stronger of the two, Alek's desire to broaden his knowledge did not remain unnoticed, and he, like Revan, eventually sought additional training from the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Zhar Lestin]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Sometime before the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3964 BBY]], both young men completed their training and attained the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]].''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' ===The Mandalorian Wars=== ====Recruiting for the Revanchists==== {{Quote|It's ridiculous, don't you see? The Sith threat ended what, 30 years ago? The Mandalorians are the threat. The Mandalorians are ''here''…almost. We need every able-bodied Jedi we can get.|Alek, to Zayne Carrick|}} In 3964 BBY, around the time both Alek and Revan were promoted to the rank of Jedi Knight, the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] launched an assault on the Republic and started the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Both Alek and Revan were eager to defeat the invading forces. However, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] urged the Jedi to be patient and strictly forbade action against the Mandalorians, feeling that the true threat had not yet emerged. Revan would not be dissuaded, and both he and Alek believed that the Order operated too slowly and exercised excessive caution in its decisions. Alek was the first to join Revan's movement, known as the [[Revanchist]]s, and other Jedi soon followed. Alek and the Revanchists began participating in the war effort through a [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] mission along the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. Led by Revan, whom Alek called "his honorary Master," Alek and his fellow Jedi investigated the Mandalorian threat along the Republic border against the wishes of the Jedi Council.[[Knights of the Old Republic 9|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 9]] [[File:ZayneMalak2.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Alek and Zayne Carrick on Taris]] The Revanchists stopped briefly on the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of Taris with hopes of recruiting more Jedi from the [[Jedi Tower|local Jedi temple]]. While there, Jedi Master [[Lucien Draay]] sent Alek to look for [[Zayne Carrick]], Draay's Padawan. Alek rescued Carrick—who was falling into the cityscape after attempting to capture the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[black marketeer]] [[Marn Hierogryph]]—by jumping off his [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] and catching the Padawan. After the rescue, the two Jedi had a conversation regarding the ongoing war. Alek, who introduced himself to Carrick as "Squint," tried to recruit Carrick to the Revanchists. However, the Padawan refused, claiming that the Taris Jedi Masters believed that the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], not the Mandalorians, were the Jedi's business. Though Carrick was a less-than-promising student, Alek suspected that he would have a significant role during the war. Before he and the Revanchists left Taris, Alek shared this opinion with Carrick and advised him that it was sometimes necessary to enter the darkness in order to save the light. After Alek and the Revanchists left Taris, however, Lucien Draay and his fellow Jedi Masters of the secret Jedi organization known as the [[Jedi Covenant|Covenant]] acted on a [[Rogue Moon Prophecy|vision]],[[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] [[Padawan Massacre|murdering]] their Padawans[[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]] in an attempt to prevent the rise of a potential [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. The Covenanters framed Carrick, who fled from the Masters, for the crime, and Carrick was subsequently forced to hide from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and [[Taris Civil Authority|civil authorities]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 2]] ====Flashpoint Station==== {{Quote|What are you doing? You can barely stand!"''
"''These are trials that only a Jedi can survive, Jarael. And I think we both know I'm the only Jedi in this conversation."''
"''How did you know—?"''
"''Because we ''do'' have abilities they have yet to discover. Maybe that'll be their undoing.|Jarael and Alek on Flashpoint Station|}} [[File:Jarael-Alek-Flashpoint.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Alek, Jarael, and his comrades at Demagol's mercy]] Soon after leaving Taris, Alek and his companions were left behind on the planet [[Suurja]] while Revan investigated events on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] and its jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]]. Roughly a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] after his meeting with Carrick on Taris, Alek and his fellow Revanchists were captured, in an [[Ambush on Suurja|ambush]] that preceded the [[Fourth battle of Suurja|fourth battle]] of Suurja, by the Mandalorians[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] and were taken to [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station|Flashpoint Station]], an old Republic research [[Space station/Legends|station]] that the Mandalorians had captured a few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] prior. There, Alek became the favorite test subject of the Mandalorian [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Demagol]], who was attempting to divine the source and nature of the Jedi's [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]]. Demagol performed various tests on Alek, including irradiation, electroshock and stress tests on a [[Torture/Legends|torture]] rack. Alek was able to use the Force to block the pain that Demagol inflicted on him,{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/demagol/index.html?page=1|text="The Secret Journal of Doctor Demagol"|int=The Secret Journal of Doctor Demagol (article)|newhost=Suvudu}} although he lost his hair as a result of the scientist's experiments.[[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]] Weeks later, a female [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|offshoot]] named [[Jarael]], who had helped Carrick elude the Jedi Masters on Taris, arrived on the station as a [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]], and Alek immediately knew the Mandalorians had mistaken her for a Jedi. When Demagol came to take Jarael, Alek volunteered to be studied in her place, insisting that he had some abilities that Demagol had yet to discover. After Alek was brought back to the holding area, Demagol intended to waste no time in testing Jarael, but Carrick and the rest of the crew of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[The Last Resort (starship)|''Last Resort'']] arrived to rescue her. Carrick pretended to have been captured by the rogue Mandalorian [[Rohlan Dyre]] and was taken in Jarael's stead by Demagol. ====A false attack and Cathar mission==== {{Quote|Master—!''"
"''I feel it! I feel it!|Alek and "the Revanchist," sensing the Serroco attack|[[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 15]]}} When Dyre and Demagol emerged, they announced that Carrick was dead. However, after the Mandalorians were tricked into leaving the station during a false "Republic attack" masterminded by Marn Hierogryph, who had allied himself with Carrick, it was revealed that Carrick was posing as Demagol, whom Dyre had managed to incapacitate. Before he and the rest of the freed Jedi prisoners escaped, Alek asked Carrick to join them once again. Carrick declined, claiming that there was something he had to finish. Alek said his good-byes to Jarael and took the unconscious Demagol to Coruscant for trial. En route to the galactic capital, however, Demagol fell into a drug-induced [[Coma/Legends|coma]] and could not be awakened to stand trial. [[File:Hoodedjediandalek.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Alek, Ferroh, and their "Master," Revan, sensed a great disturbance in the Force on Serroco.]] As the Mandalorians launched a full-scale invasion of the Republic, Revan, who was known to the public as "The Revanchist," could no longer restrain himself and openly rebelled against the Jedi Council. Alek, now Revan's right-hand man''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' traveled to worlds such as Dantooine to recruit even more Jedi to his and Revan's growing rebellion.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' While on a mission to the planet [[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]] to investigate the truth about a [[Battle of Cathar|massacre]] that had taken place there, Alek, Revan, and the Jedi accompanying them [[Force sense/Legends|felt]] death in the Force when the Mandalorians unleashed a [[Battle of Serroco|devastating nuclear attack]] on the [[Stereb]] homeworld [[Serroco]]. ====Exogorth auction==== {{Quote|This is an advance that transcends simple monetary compensation, don't you agree?''"
"''A ''dangerous'' advance. The power to destroy matter on an astronomical scale—that's too much for ''any'' government to wield responsibly!|Arkoh Adasca and Alek|}} [[File:Alek-Revanchist-representative.png|thumb|left|300px|Alek was sent by Revan to bid on the exogorths owned by Adascorp.]] After the Mandalorian's destruction of Serroco, [[Arkoh Adasca]], the eighth [[Lord/Legends|lord]] of the [[House of Adasca]] and the leader of the [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp Medical Corporation]], invited Revan to attend a meeting to bid on [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorth]] creatures, after Adasca discovered that they could be constructed into biological [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]]. Alek was sent by Revan—who had remained on Cathar to search for evidence pertaining to the Mandalorian massacre[[Star Wars Insider 110|''Star Wars Insider'' 110]]; in the "Comics" section of the magazine, [[John Jackson Miller]] says that [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] has been on [[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]] since the [[Knights of the Old Republic 15|fifteenth issue]].—to the [[Omonoth system/Legends|Omonoth system]] as a representative for the Revanchists. Prior to Alek's departure, several Jedi had experienced visions regarding the possible importance of Adasca's exogorths and the role they could play in the ongoing conflict. Alek was instructed by Revan to ensure that this new danger did not affect the war. Upon his arrival aboard the ''[[Arkanian Legacy]]'', Adasca's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], Alek was reunited with both Jarael and Dyre. Alek confided to Jarael that he did not believe Zayne Carrick was guilty of the murder charges that had been levied against him for the Padawan Massacre of Taris. Jarael, who had spent the past few days in Adasca's company, attempted to inform Alek that Adasca was scheming, but she was quickly interrupted and restrained from doing so by Adasca. Later, aboard the ''Legacy'', Adasca revealed his plan to sell his exogorth superweapons to the highest bidder. Alek reacted with disgust, feeling that such a force was too powerful to be entrusted to any government. However, he felt that they could be entrusted to the Jedi, as the Revanchists saw [[Heart/Legends|heartache]] and chaos in the years to come. As [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]], who was representing the Republic, attempted to further converse with Adasca, Alek and the Republic officers [[Carth Onasi]] and [[Dallan Morvis]] reacted in shock upon learning that [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], the leader of the Mandalorians, had also been invited by Adasca to bid on the exogorths. ====Adasca's death==== {{Quote|You'll be the one who's dead, Mandalore! I don't know why you're here—but your filthy war ends now!''"
"''What is this, Adasca? We haven't crossed the battlefront to be pestered by children—though I'll gladly dispatch this one!''"
"''You're welcome to try!|Alek and Mandalore the Ultimate|[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]]}} [[File:Karathmeetmandalore.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Mandalore swiftly defeated Alek in a skirmish aboard the ''Arkanian Legacy''.]] As [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] entered the ''Arkanian Legacy'', Alek ignited his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and leaped forward in an attempt to kill Mandalore and end the war. Predicting the attack, Mandalore quickly struck him down with his [[Mandalore the Ultimate's battle-axe|newly made battle axe]], knocking the young Jedi off his feet. While Mandalore and Adasca discussed the exogorth deal, Alek tried to convince Karath to arrest them, but the Admiral would not take the risk of doing so while they were outnumbered. As the invited representatives proposed their offers, Alek expressed disbelief that Mandalore would offer Adasca a powerful position in the Mandalorian war effort in exchange for the device controlling the exogorths. He was further surprised when Karath offered Republic territory for the device. Eventually, Lucien Draay—who had arrived on the ''Legacy'' earlier in order to investigate Adasca[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]]—and Carrick met up with Carth Onasi and Dyre to work together to create a plan to compromise Adasca's scheme. When Carrick initiated a diversion—which involved leading the Mandalorians into believing that Adasca, the Republic officers, and Jedi at the meeting set a trap for them—Alek immediately realized what was going on and participated in the [[First Battle of Omonoth|brawl]] that erupted. Not long afterward, Alek, Carrick, Draay, Onasi, Karath, Jarael, Dyre, and Morvis, were forced to escape the ''Legacy''{{'s}} observation dome as the exogorths, which had gone out of Adasca's control, devoured it and killed Adasca.[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]] ====Aiding Taris==== {{Quote|You're good. Better than you have any right to be.''"
"''And she can be better still. A warrior can tell.|Alek and Jarael, after sparring|[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]]}} [[File:Alek-Jarael-Training.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Alek sparring with Jarael]] While Karath, Morvis, and Onasi escaped on the latter's [[Deadweight|ship]], the others searched for another vessel, since Alek's ship had been destroyed in one of the [[Hangar/Legends|docking bays]]. Before Draay could arrest Carrick, the starship ''[[Moomo Williwaw]]'' crashed into the ''Arkanian Legacy'', preventing Draay from apprehending his former Padawan. Alek traveled with Carrick and his crew in the ''Williwaw'' so that the fugitive could rendezvous with Marn Hierogryph on Taris. After arriving on Taris, which had been [[Taris Siege|captured]] by the Mandalorians, Carrick rocketed down to the [[City/Legends|city]] below using a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] to aid the [[Taris Resistance|local resistance]] against the Mandalorians and meet up with Hierogryph. After this, Alek and the crew flew off in the ''Williwaw''.[[Knights of the Old Republic 22|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 22]] Alek later engaged in a sparring match with Jarael, in which she bested him. The match brought the two closer together, with Alek explaining to Jarael that the Jedi had refrained from emotional relationships since the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]; however, he stated that he did not agree with this sentiment. Although he was interested in her romantically, Jarael was reluctant to enter into a relationship with him. ====Rescue on Jebble==== {{Quote|Alek!''"
"''Come on, Zayne!|Carrick and Alek, during the rescue|[[Knights of the Old Republic 28|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 28]]}} [[File:AlekAndGangRescuingCarrickAndHierogryph.png|thumb|right|300px|Alek, Jarael, and "Dyre" rescuing Carrick and Hierogryph.]] Alek, along with Jarael, "Dyre,"—secretly Demagol—and the ''Williwaw''{{'s}} crew, eventually landed on the planet [[Jebble]] to rescue Carrick and Marn Hierogryph, who had left Taris with Jedi Knight and Covenant [[Covenant Shadow|agent]] [[Celeste Morne]] to pursue an [[Muur Talisman|ancient Sith amulet]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 25|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 25]] While the group was departing from Jebble, a Mandalorian [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] commanded by Mandalore's primary strategist [[Cassus Fett]] arrived. In order to stop a [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|transmuting disease]] that was affecting the Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] from spreading off-world, Carrick sent a message to Fett, warning him not to land his [[Troop/Legends|troops]] on the surface. Fett responded to this by ordering a [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear]] [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]] of the planet. Made more determined by the loss of Morne, who had seemingly died during the [[Disaster on Jebble|bombing]], Carrick reminded Alek that he owed him a favor from Flashpoint Station and asked him, along with the rest of the ''Williwaw''{{'s}} crew, for assistance in stopping the Jedi Covenant. Before they separated once more, Alek told Carrick, who engineered a plan to stop the Covenant, to bring evidence of the Covenant's [[Sanctum of the Exalted|Sith artifact warehouse]] to the Jedi High Council. Alek and the Taris Resistance fighter [[Shel Jelavan]], whose [[Shad Jelavan|brother]] had been killed by the Covenant in the Padawan Massacre, went to Coruscant to perform their part of Carrick's plan. ====Captain Malak==== {{Dialogue|Vandar Tokare|Be honest! How hard would it be to create shadow agents with that wealth? You've complained yourself—Jedi are reported killed on remote missions all the time without investigations. And ''Krynda Draay'' has trained Jedi for ''thirty years'' without our supervision. You know how thin we were after the Sith War—we were happy for the help! How many remain loyal to her now?|Alek|Is anything impossible when the forces opposing it are asleep?|Vrook Lamar|You would know something about Jedi operating without leave, I'd bet—but never mind. Even with what Shel has told us of ''Raana Tey'', we could never bring this to the Council. You don't know what Krynda means to the Jedi, son. She's one of the few Sith War heroes we have left. You accuse her, you won't rock the Order. You'll tear it apart!|attr=Alek discusses Krynda Draay and her Jedi Covenant with Jedi Masters Vrook Lamar and Vandar Tokare|src=}} [[File:ShelMalakTokare-KotOR31.png|thumb|left|200px|"Captain Malak" and Shel Jelavan met with Vandar Tokare on Coruscant.]] Due to the arrest warrant that had been issued for the Revanchists by the Jedi Council, Alek went under the guise of one "[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Malak" and adorned his head with [[Color/Legends|blue]]–gray [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]]. After sending a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] transmission to Masters [[Vandar Tokare]] and [[Vrook Lamar]] on Coruscant to arrange a meeting, Alek and Jelavan rendezvoused with the two Masters inside a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|underlevels]] of Coruscant to discuss evidence pertaining to the Padawan Massacre. Following their testimony, the foursome arrived at the rendezvous point where they were to meet with Carrick and Hierogryph. Despite Alek's assurances that Carrick's name would be cleared, Lamar stated that if they accused [[Krynda Draay]], the founder of the Covenant, of playing a part in any of the Covenant's wrongdoing, it would tear the Order apart. Lamar and Tokare stressed that what they needed was one of the Covenanters to stand up and confess. As Alek and Jelavan were discussing the Covenant's actions with Lamar and Tokare, Covenant Jedi Master [[Xamar]] arrived with Carrick and Hierogryph, and agreed to testify against the Covenant's illegal activities before the Council.[[Knights of the Old Republic 32|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 32]] Later on, Alek testified to the Council on Carrick's behalf following the events of the [[Vindication|Covenant Affair]], the Covenant's failed insurrection against the Jedi Order. Alek was then sent to the Revanchists with a final warning to cease their participation in the Mandalorian Wars. He went under protest, determined to retain the name "Malak" until there was an end to the war's suffering.[[Knights of the Old Republic 35|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 35]] ====Vision on Cathar==== {{Quote|We began with nothing but a leader and a purpose—now, we have an army. And ''sanction''! The High Council fell into line as soon as word spread—and the Republic is overjoyed!|Malak, to Jarael, Marn Hierogryph, [[Slyssk]], and [[T1-LB]]|}} [[File:RevanIsBorn-KotOR42.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Malak and his fellow Jedi look on as Revan vows to defeat the Mandalorians on Cathar]] After Malak left Coruscant and returned to Cathar, where Revan had been attempting to find proof of the Mandalorians' devastation of the Cathar people, Vrook Lamar and a group of Jedi tracked Malak and Revan to the planet. Lamar and the Jedi met with Revan, Malak, and the Revanchists and told the group to abandon their efforts and disperse permanently, as the Jedi Masters believed that the Mandalorians were not a threat and that nothing warranted the Jedi's involvement in the Mandalorian Wars. During the discussion, Revan noticed a [[Revan's mask|Mandalorian helmet]] underneath his feet and picked it up. When some light struck the helmet, all the Jedi present experienced a vision of the past of Mandalorians driving hordes of Cathar into the [[Ocean/Legends|sea]]. [[Unidentified Mandalorian female|A Mandalorian female]], to whom the helmet in Revan's hands belonged, tried to convince Cassus Fett—who had led the attack on the planet—that the Cathar had been defeated and did not need to all be killed. Fett refused to stand down, saying that the Cathar left a stain of dishonor amongst the Mando'ade and that he intended to wash that stain clean by committing [[Speciecide|genocide]]. Fett then ordered the planet's devastation, killing the Cathar and the Mandalorian female. After the vision's end, Malak and the Revanchists witnessed as Revan pledged to defeat the Mandalorians. Although the truth of what happened on Cathar was known, the Jedi still did not give Revan and Malak their full support, but Revan managed to organize the Revanchists under the banner of a [[Republic Mercy Corps]]. The Jedi Council subsequently allowed Revan, Malak, and the Revanchists to participate in the Mandalorian Wars.[[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 49]] Both Malak and Revan were promoted to the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|general]]. Malak eventually contacted Marn Hierogryph in search of Jarael, and the Snivvian informed Malak that the two of them, along with a few others, were on the planet [[Wor Tandell/Legends|Wor Tandell]]. Intending to surprise Jarael, Malak went to the planet and met her by her crew's [[Hot Prospect|ship]]. Later that night, Malak told Jarael and Hierogryph that the Jedi Council had officially sanctioned the Revanchist Jedi's involvement in the Mandalorian Wars. ====Skirmish with "Rohlan Dyre"==== {{Quote|Stop! Rohlan is our friend!''"
"''They're not capable of friendship, Jarael! Only torture—and genocide! Like Cassus Fett and Demagol! If you'd been on Cathar, you'd know!|Jarael and Malak, regarding "Dyre" and the Mandalorians|}} [[File:MalakRohmagolFight.jpg|thumb|left|400px|Malak confronts "Rohlan Dyre"]] Malak told Jarael that the Revanchists had almost everything they needed to win the war, and asked that she bring her combat abilities and firsthand experience with the Mandalorians' atrocities to their cause. When Hierogryph mentioned Jarael's newly discovered Force talents, Malak became excited, telling Jarael that she belonged with him. Dyre then appeared, shouting that Malak could never take her. When Malak attempted to reason with the man, the Mandalorian punched him in the jaw. Malak ignited his lightsaber and began attacking Dyre, who evaded him with his jetpack and fired at the Jedi with his [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. Jarael screamed at Malak to stop, but he refused, reasoning that the Mandalorians were incapable of friendship, only torture, and genocide. Malak eventually cut Dyre's blasters in half and wrestled him to the ground, asking the Mandalorian if he had been there when the Mandalorians had killed the Cathar. At that moment, Republic captain [[Telettoh]] and the Cathar Jedi [[Ferroh]] arrived at Carrick's camp along with the former Padawan. They pulled Malak off Dyre, calming the Jedi down. Telettoh berated Malak for attacking Dyre, stating that the Revanchists could not afford any scandals. Carrick apologized to Malak for not arriving at the camp sooner, expressing his astonishment at the fact that Malak had brought the Republic [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Testament]]'' just to see Carrick and his friends. Malak informed Carrick that he was there for Jarael, and Carrick pretended that he and Jarael were romantically involved, which surprised Malak. Having failed to enlist Jarael, Malak bid goodbye to Carrick, Jarael, and their crew. Eventually, Malak was contacted by Jarael, who asked for his assistance in tracking down Carrick, who had been kidnapped by the slavery organization know as [[The Crucible]] during a mission to rescue the Crucible's slaves. However, Malak could not offer his aid since he and the Revanchists were fighting the Mandalorians.[[Knights of the Old Republic 46|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 46]] ====The trial of Demagol==== =====Out of coma===== {{Quote|In the name of the millions the Mandalorians have killed, tortured, and enslaved—we'll save the Republic! And defeat the Mandalorians forever!"''
''"This—this is your secret, isn't it? You can't beat us in a ''fair fight''! That's the way you ''like'' it, Jedi—isn't it? So save your Republic—but who'll save the Republic from ''you''?|Malak and Rohlan Dyre|[[Knights of the Old Republic 47|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 47]]}} [[File:DemagolMalakTrial.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Malak interrogates Rohlan Dyre, mistaken as Demagol, in front of the audience in the Senate chamber on Coruscant]] Eventually, Malak and the Republic forces under his command recaptured the Omonoth system and recovered the ''Arkanian Legacy''{{'s}} remains. Sometime after this, Demagol came out of his drug-induced coma;[[Knights of the Old Republic 45|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 45]] however, the man whom both Malak and the Republic believed to be Demagol was in actuality Rohlan Dyre. The Mandalorian deserter had been drugged on Flashpoint Station by Demagol, who had switched [[Armor/Legends|armors]] with Dyre so that he could travel with Jarael, Carrick, and their companions. Malak participated in a [[Trial of Demagol|show trial]] for Demagol, which was set at Coruscant's [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] [[Grand Convocation Chamber|chamber]]. Before Malak testified against Demagol, he met with Zayne Carrick and Marn Hierogryph, who had also been called to testify against the scientist. When Malak inquired as to where Jarael was, Carrick told him that she had left his group. Malak informed Carrick and Hierogryph that after he testified he would be going back to the front lines of the war, and that the ship under his command was already with Admiral Karath's patrol. He also informed them about the recovery of the ''Arkanian Legacy''. =====The wrong Mandalorian===== {{Quote|You're all a bunch of incompetent clowns! Demagol never got back to lockup! You know who we're looking for—stop every vehicle you find! I don't care about rights or jurisdiction—there's a monster on the loose!|Malak, ordering Republic forces to find "Demagol"|}} [[File:MalakAngryAtRepublicForces.png|thumb|left|300px|Malak was angry at Republic forces for letting "Demagol" escape.]] During the trial, Malak testified to the audience about Demagol's experiments on both him and the Jedi who had not survived the scientist's studies. As Malak continued, Dyre, who insisted that the Jedi stop calling him by the name Demagol, broke free of his [[Binders/Legends|binders]]. Malak used the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|throw]] Dyre across the chamber, and publicly vowed to save the Republic by defeating the Mandalorians permanently, in the name of the millions whom the Mandalorians had slain, tormented, or subjugated. Dyre openly questioned Malak's tactics, asking who would save the Republic from Malak after the Jedi succeeded in his quest. The Senate guards escorted Dyre out of the Senate chamber and put the Mandalorian in a [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]] which would take him back to his holding cell. However, the two guards in the speeder were Zayne Carrick and Marn Hierogryph, who had figured out that Demagol switched places with Rohlan Dyre. After learning that "Demagol" had not made it to his holding cell, Malak headed the search for the scientist on Coruscant and ordered his forces to detain and search every speeder they found.[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]] Not long after, the true Demagol was killed, but Malak, unaware of that,[[Knights of the Old Republic 50|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 50]] continued his search for Demagol.{{DHBoards|url=Help/Boards/viewtopic.php?p=238483|thread=KNIGHTS OF THE OLD REPUBLIC #50 -- My only friend, the end!|user=John Jackson Miller|PTime=[[February 17]], [[2010]]}} ====Later Mandalorian battles==== {{Quote|Revan and Malak were heroes, the great saviors of the Republic.|Dorak|audio=DorakRevanandMalak.flac|}} The entrance of Revan and his Jedi into the war marked the conflict's turning point. Revan's military strategies and tactics, coupled with Malak's courage and determination, saw the pair become the foremost heroes of the war. Although the [[Battle of Duro (Mandalorian Wars)|Battle of Duro]] was a disaster, a recent breakthrough in [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] had resulted in the construction of the [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruiser]], and Revan and Malak were able to effectively use the abilities of a fleet of these new ships to prevent the Mandalorians from escaping with stocks of material from the [[Duro Starshipwright Shipyards|Duro shipyards]].''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' [[File:Malakrevan-JMGD.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Revan and Malak took control of the entire Republic Navy.]] Public opinion of the pair, combined with his desire to register his displeasure with the Jedi Council, motivated [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Tol Cressa]] to give Revan direct control over the entire [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic fleet]]. With his newfound authority, Revan spearheaded the Republic war effort and began to steadily push the Mandalorians back, and though Malak lacked Revan's military genius, he exemplified himself as a capable war leader. Malak eventually earned a reputation for being a headstrong warrior that charged into danger recklessly.{{InsiderCite|100|The 100 Greatest Things About Star Wars... Ever!|The 100 Greatest Things About ''Star Wars''... Ever!}} Together, Revan and Malak [[Liberation of Taris|liberated]] Taris, [[Battle of Jaga's Cluster|fought]] Cassus Fett at [[Jaga's Cluster/Legends|Jaga's Cluster]], and [[Battle of Althir (second)|defeated]] much of the Mandalorian [[Army/Legends|army]] at the Outer Rim world [[Althir III]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Yet, despite such victories, the Jedi Masters whom they had left behind believed that Revan, Malak, and the Jedi that had joined them were only offering easy solutions to the galaxy's problems, fearing that many woes would result from their recklessness. As the war progressed, concerns were raised over Revan and Malak's behavior. They gradually changed from the idealistic men that had first rebelled against the Jedi Council and began to adopt characteristics similar to their Mandalorian adversaries. To Revan and Malak, ultimate victory became all that mattered, and in order to ensure it, the two Jedi met their enemies' brutal, merciless tactics with a ruthlessness of their own. Over the course of the war, Malak and Revan developed a cold, calculating disposition that was uncharacteristic of Jedi and grew to despise weakness and indecisiveness. "Moral shortcuts" became common practice under their leadership, along with a number of other unsavory acts. Though there were some who frowned upon such actions, the vast majority of the galaxy cared only that Revan and Malak were winning the war and hailed them as heroes. ====Dantooine Star Map and Malachor V==== {{Quote|The dark side is strong in this place—I can feel its power! Is this wise? The ancient Jedi sealed this archway. If we pass beyond this door, we can never go back. The Order will surely banish us. Are the secrets of the Star Forge so valuable? Can its power truly be worth the risk?|Malak warning Revan before they found the Dantooine Star Map|audio=MalakDantooineShrine.flac|}} [[File:Revanfindsmap1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Revan and Malak find the first Star Map in the ancient ruins on Dantooine]] At some point during the Mandalorian Wars, Revan found a [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]] in the old [[Rakatan ruins (Dantooine)|ruins]] near the Jedi Enclave on Dantooine. The Star Map was an integral part to uncovering the long-lost location of the [[Star Forge]], a [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-based war relic of the Rakatan [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] that had ruled the galaxy approximately 20,000 standard years before, which was fueled by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and capable of quickly constructing starships. Revan and Malak delved into the ancient ruins, and after successfully passing a set of trials barring their way, they were given access to the Star Map. Moments before they crossed the threshold that would lead them to their prize, Malak warned Revan of the consequences, feeling that if they examined the Star Map, the Jedi Order would banish both him and Revan. However, such words did not dissuade Revan from continuing to the Star Map. Although a part of Malak yearned to turn back and return to the Order, he remained at his friend's side, knowing that once he crossed the threshold, there would be no chance of ever returning to the life he once had. A [[3961 BBY|year before]] the end of the Mandalorian Wars, Revan found another Star Map on the Wookiee homeworld of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. Shortly thereafter, Revan and Malak traveled to the Sith planet of [[Malachor V]], where they discovered the [[Trayus Academy]] and delved deeply into the Sith secrets contained within. It was there that Revan introduced Malak to the Sith teachings, causing him to fall to the dark side. The heroic reputation that Revan and Malak had gained during the Mandalorian Wars was finally cemented during the [[Battle of Malachor V|final battle]] of the war, in which Revan forced a final confrontation with the Mandalorians over Malachor V.{{LucasArts|url=games/swkotor_sithlords|text=''Chronicles of the Old Republic''|int=Chronicles of the Old Republic}} During the battle, Revan slew Mandalore the Ultimate, taking his [[Mandalore's Mask|mask]] and learning of a [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith threat]] behind the Mandalorian Wars. Mandalore's fall forced the Mandalorians into unconditional surrender. ====Uncovering Nathema==== {{Quote|What?''"
"''Are you sure it's here?''"
"''It's here, I can feel it.''"
"''Maybe it's on the other side.''"
"''It's this one, The entrance is around here somewhere.''"
"''Here! I found it!|Revan and Malak, searching for Dramath the Second's tomb|}} [[File:JediExileTOR.png|thumb|right|200px|Malak wanted to have Meetra Surik (pictured) killed but Revan refused.]] After the cataclysmic final battle, Jedi General [[Meetra Surik]] left Revan and Malak's fleet to answer for her actions before the Jedi Council. Malak wanted Revan to order the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[HK-47]], whom Revan had recently constructed, to kill her. However, Revan would not allow her assassination. Having defeated the Mandalorians, the two now-legendary Jedi Knights did not return to the Republic. Instead, they led the remainder of their forces into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], hunting down surviving Mandalorians. For a year afterward, nothing was heard from them, and the Republic feared its heroes were lost. In the three [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the battle at Malachor V, Malak and Revan traveled to the frigid planet of [[Rekkiad]] to search for [[Dramath the Second]]'s [[Dramath the Second's tomb|tomb]]. They discovered the Sith Lord's tomb hidden in a giant [[First Spear|pillar of ice]] and found a [[Dramath the Second's datacron|datacron]] that would lead them to the planet [[Nathema]]. So as to condemn the Mandalorians to wander without a leader, Revan left Mandalore's mask in the tomb before he and Malak left for Nathema. When the two Jedi arrived there, they found the planet [[Sith Emperor's ritual|devoid]] of the Force. They searched through the empty buildings looking for information that would guide them closer to finding the origin of the Sith threat. ===Sith apprentice=== ====Sith Emperor and Rakata encounter==== {{Quote|When they left after the Mandalorian Wars ended, they were Jedi. When they returned… they were something else.|Carth Onasi, regarding Revan and Malak|audio=CarthRevanLeft.flac|}} [[File:Timeline8.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Fallen Jedi Revan and Malak proclaim themselves the new Dark Lords of the Sith]] During their mission in the Unknown Regions, Revan and Malak encountered the Sith Empire on the Imperial capital of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], and learned of the Dark Lord of the Sith known only as the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] that led it. While there, the two Jedi posed as mercenaries for months, attempting to learn anything and everything about the [[Sith Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. They [[Mission to Dromund Kaas|found]] out that the Emperor was raising an army to again [[Great Galactic War|invade the Republic]], and eventually located a [[Yarri|member]] of the [[Imperial Guard]] who agreed to sneak them into the [[Imperial Citadel (Sith Empire)|Imperial Citadel]]; however they were unaware of the Imperial Guard's bond to the Emperor's will and were instead led into a trap. Revan and Malak attempted to fight the Emperor but were overwhelmed by his power. He broke their will, twisted their minds and granted them the "Darth" title and sent them back to act as the vanguard of his invasion and report back when all resistance was crushed. Their mission was to recover the Rakatan Star Maps and locate the Star Forge. He intended to use the Star Forge to accelerate the construction of his Sith armada, which would enable the Sith to defeat the Jedi and the Republic centuries ahead of schedule. However, the Emperor underestimated the power and strength of their will and minds. Revan and Malak believed that they were acting of their own accord and, in doing so, were able to block their memories of the Sith and the Emperor. Revan and Malak located three additional Star Maps on the desert world [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath's]] [[Water/Legends|oceanic]] homeworld [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]], and the planet Korriban. [[File:BlackRakata-KOTOR.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Malak and Revan were attacked by the Black Rakata on Lehon.]] Examining all five Star Maps, Revan and Malak eventually discovered and [[Mission to Rakata Prime|traveled]] to the previously unexplored [[Lehon system]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' where the Star Forge was located, drawing energy from the system's [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Abo]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' identifies the [[Star/Legends|star]] of the [[Lehon system]], which the [[Star Forge]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbits]] in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', as [[Abo]]. Upon their arrival, the pair crashed on the Rakatan homeworld [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]] as the result of a [[disruptor field]] which was protecting the Star Forge. Shortly thereafter, they were attacked by the primitive [[Black Rakata]], who attempted to take them prisoner, but after the two Sith Lords displayed their Force powers, the natives were quickly cowed into submission. Though Revan initially negotiated with [[The One]], the leader of the Black Rakata, it soon became apparent that none among the Black Rakata had relevant information pertaining to the Star Forge. Revan then decided to win the favor of the [[Elders (Rakatan)|Elders]], a Rakatan tribe that the Black Rakata was at war with. Unlike their violent introduction to the Black Rakata, Revan and Malak approached the Elders in peace, learning much from them about the Rakata, their old empire, and of the disruptor field that had caused the two Sith to crash. ====Star Forge found and Jedi Civil War begins==== {{Quote|The Star Forge is more than just a space station. In some ways, it is like a living creature. It hungers. And it can feed on the dark side that is within all of us.|Darth Malak|audio=MalakStarForgeNature.flac|}} Becoming increasingly aware of the abhorrence that the Elders held for their [[Species/Legends|species]]' violent past, Revan and Malak styled themselves as servants of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] that were searching for a way to destroy the Star Forge. After Revan and Malak gained the Elders' trust, the Elders gave the Sith access to the [[Temple of the Ancients]], which enabled Revan and Malak to disable the disruptor field and reach the Star Forge. Thanking the Elders, the two Sith Lords promised to destroy the Star Forge and return. After finding the Star Forge, Revan and Malak began to have visions of their own Sith Empire. Revan took control of the Star Forge and declared himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith, with Malak grudgingly taking the role of his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. In [[3959 BBY]], the pair founded a new [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] and returned to Korriban, seizing control of the planet and re-establishing the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] there. Revan and Malak then returned to known Republic space at the head of a massive invasion fleet, comprised of Jedi Knights and Republic soldiers that were veterans of the Mandalorian Wars. [[File:Malak_Jawless.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Malak with his protective jaw-casing removed]] Openly revealing themselves as Sith Lords, the pair and their followers declared war on the Republic and the Jedi Order. This marked the beginning of a planned twenty-year campaign to end the "tyranny" of the Jedi Council once and for all,{{InsiderCite|88|Heritage of the Sith}} initiating the Jedi Civil War. At one point under Darth Revan's tutelage, Malak openly viewed his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] as being soft, causing tension between the two that [[Duel on Revan's flagship|culminated]] in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]]. Revan removed Malak's jaw with a lightsaber strike, and Malak was forced to wear a large metal [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthesis]] cover over where his jaw had once been. A vocabulator allowed Malak to talk, but his voice now reverberated with a gruff electronic tone.{{CSWECite|II|279|Malak, Darth}} In [[3958 BBY]], Revan and his Sith carried out a successful [[Battle of Foerost (Jedi Civil War)|attack]] on the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] world of [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]], capturing the majority of the Republic's fleet. Admiral Saul Karath, who had served under Revan and Malak in the Mandalorian Wars, declared the Republic unworthy of his loyalty and transferred his services and his flagship, the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], to Revan's service. During this time, Malak gained ownership of the ''Leviathan'', taking it as his own flagship. Karath also provided security codes that enabled the Sith to bypass a Republic [[Navy/Legends|Navy]] docking bay's scanners. This allowed the Sith forces to sweep in and destroy over half of the docked ships before they had time to react. Later, Revan promoted Karath as the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the entire Sith fleet. ====Telos IV bombardment and usurping Darth Revan==== {{Quote|I ordered my own ships to fire on your bridge. I thought I could destroy all my enemies with a single glorious victory! I never dreamed the Jedi would take you alive from the wreckage.|Darth Malak, to Revan, on betraying him|audio=MalakFiredonRevan.flac|}} Darth Malak ordered Karath to [[Bombing of Telos IV|destroy]] [[Telos IV]], a planet that Revan had originally intended to conquer, in order to test the Admiral's loyalty to the Sith. Karath complied with Malak's command, killing millions of innocent people and earning the wrath of the surviving Telosians, including the Republic commander Carth Onasi, who had once considered Karath his mentor and Malak a hero. Later that year, Darth Malak [[Battle of Iridonia (Jedi Civil War)|bombed]] the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] homeworld of [[Iridonia/Legends|Iridonia]]''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' and carried out a [[Battle of Rodia|successful attack]] on the planet of [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]]. Though a powerful Sith Lord, the ambitious Darth Malak began to chafe under the dominion of Revan as the war progressed. The man who had once been Revan's staunchest ally and most loyal friend began to eagerly await an opportunity to replace his Sith Master. [[File:Malak-betrays-Revan.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Darth Malak watches as his cruiser fires on Darth Revan's flagship]] Malak's chance to usurp Revan's position came in [[3957 BBY]]''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' establishes that [[Darth Glovoc]] received a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the [[Luire system]] in [[3957 BBY]], and states that his vision occurs "on the eve of Darth Malak's death at the hands of Revan." It also states that Glovoc's ship, the ''[[Dying Sun]]'', arrived in the Luire system and begins surveying the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nyriaan]] in [[3956 BBY]], which is also the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] that ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' place Darth Malak's defeat at the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]]. Additionally, the ''Campaign Guide'' states that Malak ruled [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]] for a span of a few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after [[Capture of Darth Revan|deposing]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. Thus, since ''Galaxy of Intrigue'' places Malak's defeat early in 3956 BBY, Revan's capture must have occurred several months prior, in 3957 BBY. when the Jedi Council sent a [[Jedi strike team (Capture of Darth Revan)|strike team]] to [[Capture of Darth Revan|capture]] the two Sith Lords. The team managed to corner Revan on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of [[Darth Revan's flagship|his flagship]]. Malak, who had been separated from his Master, ordered the guns of his own flagship, the ''Leviathan'', to fire on Revan's vessel in an attempt to slay both his Master and the Jedi—specifically the strike team's leader, Bastila Shan, because of her skills with [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], a Force power that enabled her to strengthen the Republic forces' resolve and coordinate their fighting effort while sapping the will of their enemies. Unbeknownst to Malak, Shan saved Revan's life and brought him to the Jedi Council, who, under the direction of Masters [[Dorak]] and Zhar Lestin, reprogrammed his mind with the identity of a Republic soldier. Though Malak failed in his attempt on Revan's life, his Master had been incapacitated during the attack. Believing himself to have been successful, Darth Malak assumed command of the Sith Empire, at last taking the mantle of Dark Lord for himself. A small part of Malak always regretted betraying his Master from afar, however. Malak knew there would be some who thought and claimed that he had been a coward, that he had acted out of fear so as not to face Revan in personal combat. ===Dark Lord of the Sith=== ====The search for Bastila Shan==== {{Quote|I am more interested in the young Jedi Bastila and her Battle Meditation. Have you learned how she escaped the destruction of Taris?|Darth Malak, to Saul Karath|audio=MalakBastila.flac|}} [[File:BecauseISaidSo-K1.png|thumb|left|300px|Darth Malak ordered Saul Karath to destroy the planet Taris.]] Although his stratagems and tactics centered on brute force, Malak won many victories over the battered and outnumbered Republic forces through the sheer size of his armadas. Unlike his former Sith Master, the Sith Magnus would destroy worlds rather than capture them, ensuring that neither the Sith nor the Republic had the resources they needed. He fueled the Star Forge, which was modified to produce the ships and assault [[Droid/Legends|droids]] of the Sith fleet, by using the Force powers of captured Jedi Knights. After Revan's apparent death, Malak took a former Jedi whose quest for power had set him apart from his fellow students and named him [[Darth Bandon]], taking him as his [[Shadow Hand]] and elevating the young Sith to the second-in-command in the Sith Empire. Jedi that were not killed by Malak's Sith forces fell from the light, becoming [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and swearing their allegiance to the Dark Lord. While the war against the Republic continued with success, Darth Malak feared that the Jedi Bastila Shan's rare gift with battle meditation—which surpassed even the most powerful Jedi Masters—would make her a grave threat to the Sith war effort. In [[3956 BBY]], Malak expended tremendous resources in an attempt to locate and capture Shan. He nearly succeeded when his apprentice, Darth Bandon, led an [[Attack on the Endar Spire|attack]] on her ship, the ''[[Endar Spire]]'', above the planet Taris. Shan, among other Republic soldiers, was forced to evacuate her craft via an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] and crashed into the lower levels of Taris. To prevent Shan from escaping, Malak [[Capture of Taris|captured]] Taris and [[Blockade/Legends|blockaded]] the planet. However, Malak, who was on board the ''Leviathan'', soon grew impatient with the search for Shan and ordered Karath to use the ''Leviathan'' to destroy the planet. Nonetheless, Shan was able to return unharmed to Dantooine on the light [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', thanks to the [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|efforts]] of Carth Onasi, the amnesiac Revan—who was unaware of his former identity as the Dark Lord—and their companions. Soon after their arrival, Revan was retrained in the Jedi ways in the Enclave on Dantooine, since he began having visions of his past and the Star Maps that led to the Star Forge. The [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|local Jedi Council]] ordered Revan, Shan, and their allies to search for the Star Forge. ====Hiring Calo Nord and Dantooine attack==== {{Quote|Calo Nord is dead, Lord Malak. He has failed in his mission. Forgive me."''
"''The penalty for failure is death, Admiral Karath. But the failure was Calo's, not yours. You may rise.|Saul Karath and Darth Malak|audio=SaulMalakCaloDead.flac|}} [[File:Malak_hires_Nord.png|thumb|right|300px|Calo Nord was hired by Darth Malak to capture Bastila Shan.]] Shortly thereafter, a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Calo Nord]]—who had been defeated on Taris by Revan, and had managed to escape Taris' destruction—was hired by Malak to track down Shan and her allies, capture her, and kill her companions. Nord and Karath revealed to Malak that one of Shan's companions was Revan, whom the Sith Lord had long believed to be dead. Despite Nord's galaxy-wide reputation as a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter, he did not survive his [[Ambush on Tatooine (Jedi Civil War)|next encounter]] with Revan. After Nord's death, Karath informed Malak of the bounty hunter's failure. Malak believed that no bounty hunter could stand against a Jedi, and to avoid making the same mistake twice, the Sith Lord sent his apprentice, Darth Bandon, after Shan. Malak ordered Bandon to find Shan and to bring her to him alive, if at all possible. There were rumors that Bandon would challenge Malak for the right to rule the Sith,{{DB|character|darthbandon|Bandon, Darth}} but Bandon never had a chance to develop his plans, as he failed to capture Shan and was [[Mission to Hrakert Station|killed]] by Revan during the quest to locate the Star Forge. During Revan's search for the Rakatan space station, Malak and his Sith forces [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|attacked]] Dantooine. Although the Jedi Council had sensed this would happen and attempted to evacuate everyone, they were unsuccessful in preventing the Enclave's destruction and Malak's attack killed many, Jedi and civilians alike. Malak also captured eight Jedi for later use on the Star Forge. The Sith Lord plundered the ruins of the Enclave, looking for [[Jedi artifact/Legends|Jedi artifacts]] but many of the Jedi [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]] and relics had been moved to another location. ====Revelation on the ''Leviathan''==== {{Quote|You cannot hide from what you once were, Revan! Recognize that you were once the Dark Lord—and know that I have taken your place!|Malak, to Revan|audio=MalakReveal.flac|}} [[File:BastilaIntervenesMalak.png|thumb|left|300px|Darth Malak fights Bastila Shan aboard the ''Leviathan'']] While on their search for the fifth and final Star Map needed to locate the Star Forge, the ''Ebon Hawk'' and its crew were captured by the Sith and brought on board the ''Leviathan''. While Malak, who was in another [[Sector/Legends|sector]] at the time, made his way to the ''Leviathan'', Karath began to torture and interrogate Revan, Shan, and Onasi in an attempt to find out their mission's objective. Sometime after Karath ended the interrogation, an ''Ebon Hawk'' crew member who had evaded capture managed to liberate Revan and the others from the detention area. The crew then split into two groups. Shan, Onasi, and Revan headed to the bridge of the ''Leviathan'' to lower the ship's shields, disable the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]], and unlock the door to the docking bay, while the rest of the crew went directly to the docking bay to prepare the ''Ebon Hawk'' for escape. By the time Malak arrived on the ship, Shan, Onasi and Revan had already disabled the tractor beam, killing Karath in the process. Malak encountered the trio on their way to the docking bay, hindering their escape. Conferring with his three adversaries, Malak discovered that Revan still had no knowledge of his time as a Dark Lord. Amused, Malak revealed to Revan the truth of his former identity, which Shan confirmed to the shocked Revan. He looked on in disgust as Revan forgave Shan for this long deception; this display of "weakness" further justified his decision to take the title of Dark Lord from Revan. At that moment, he used the Force to create a [[Force Stasis/Legends|stasis field]] around Shan and Onasi and prepared to kill his former Master. Revan proved himself to be a capable swordsman, however, holding his own against the Dark Lord before being caught in stasis, rendering him defenseless. Meanwhile, Shan and Onasi were freed from stasis, and Shan intervened so that Revan and Onasi could escape, though her actions came at the cost of being defeated and captured by Malak. [[File:Darth-Malak-Bastila-Shan-NEC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Dark Malak with his last apprentice, Bastila Shan]] After successfully capturing Shan aboard the ''Leviathan'', Malak took the Jedi to the Temple of the Ancients on Rakata Prime. Seeking to make Shan his apprentice in order to fortify the Sith Empire's efforts with her battle meditation, Malak attempted to turn her to the dark side and tortured her with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. Shan was at first firm and resisted his efforts, but Malak insisted that it was not merely torture—it was a taste of the dark side's power that she could possess. Following a week of torture, Malak succeeded in breaking Shan's will, finally turning her to the dark side, and took her as his apprentice to replace the fallen Darth Bandon. ====Battle of Rakata Prime==== =====Republic arrival===== {{Main|Battle of Rakata Prime}} {{Quote|You made a mistake coming here, Revan. The Star Forge fuels my command of the dark side. You are no match for me here. And this time you will not escape!|Darth Malak, to Revan|audio=MalakRevanMistake.flac|}} [[File:Malak_closeup.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Malak on the Star Forge]] Eventually, the Star Forge was operating at three hundred percent beyond the Sith's expectations, and the space station added new ships to the Sith fleet every day. Malak was informed by Sith Admiral [[Varko (Human)|Varko]] that the fleet was merely awaiting the Dark Lord's instructions; the Sith Lord was confident that they would be invincible once Shan provided assistance with her battle meditation. Assuming they would be victorious, Malak told Varko of his plans to begin the final conquest of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], which would crush the Republic permanently. However, Revan and his crew located the Star Forge and sent the space station's coordinates to Republic Admiral [[Forn Dodonna]] so that she could bring the Republic fleet and Jedi reinforcements led by Jedi Master Vandar Tokare to attack the Sith and their Star Forge. After Revan and the Jedi penetrated the Star Forge's defenses, Malak was not surprised, ordering [[Unidentified Dark Jedi Master (Star Forge)|one]] of his Dark Jedi to dispatch the Star Forge battle droids to deal with them. However, Malak underestimated the Jedi, and the Dark Jedi Master brought news of the droids' failure. Malak was at first surprised that any Jedi could stand against an army of Star Forge droids, but then he was told that Revan was with the Jedi. Acknowledging Revan's power, Malak dispatched his forces and Dark Jedi to hinder Revan's progress and allocate enough time for the Sith Lord himself to fully prepare the Star Forge's defenses, knowing that his Sith troops would only slow Revan down, not defeat him. =====Shan's redemption===== {{Quote|I will stay here in this chamber and use my Battle Meditation to aid the Republic fleet. I am their only hope of destroying the Star Forge and ending the Sith menace. You must go and face Malak, but you have to hurry. Once I turn the battle in the Republic's favor we won't have much time to escape the Star Forge before it is destroyed.|Bastila Shan, to Revan|audio=BastilaSFBattleMeditation.flac|}} [[File:BastilaAvatar.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Revan convinced Bastila Shan (pictured) to return to the light side.]] Malak summoned his new apprentice, Bastila Shan, and informed her that Revan was on the Star Forge. Shan was conflicted due to her love for Revan, but Malak stated that she would prove herself worthy of being his apprentice if she defeated the former Sith Lord, a mandate that Shan promised she would not fail to complete. The Dark Lord later mused that perhaps Shan would be able to defeat Revan, but even if she failed, it would provide the necessary time to prepare the Star Forge's defenses. However, Revan also convinced Shan to return to the light side and aid the Republic through her battle meditation. Malak—who was in the midst of using the Force to choke two Jedi prisoners—was confronted by Revan outside the [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] leading to the Star Forge's observation tower. Upon his former Master's arrival, the Dark Lord threw his lightsaber into one of the Jedi and blasted the other with Force lightning, killing both. Revan offered Malak a chance to surrender, reminding the Dark Lord that the Jedi might show him mercy. However, Malak replied that the wiping of an individual's identity was no act of mercy and stated that he would rather die. The Sith Lord then unleashed his unlimited army of Star Forge battle droids against Revan, using the opportunity to escape to the Star Forge's observation deck. =====Final confrontation and death===== {{Quote|Im… [cough] impossible. I… I cannot be beaten. I am the Dark Lord of the Sith."''
"''The power of the light will always be stronger than the dark side."''
"''Still… [cough] still spouting the wisdom [of] the Jedi, I see. Maybe there is more truth in their code than I ever believed. I… I cannot help but wonder, Revan. What would have happened had our positions been reversed? What if fate had decreed I would be captured by the Jedi? Could I have returned to the light, as you did? [cough] If you had not led me down the dark path in the first place, what destiny would I have found?"''
"''I am sorry I started you on this path. But you chose to continue down it."''
"''I suppose… I suppose you speak the truth. I alone must accept responsibility for my fate. I wanted to be Master of the Sith and ruler of the galaxy. But that destiny was not mine, Revan. [cough] It might have been yours, perhaps… but never mine. And in the end, as the darkness takes me, I am nothing.|Darth Malak and Revan|}} [[File:Revan_fights_Malak.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Malak confronts Revan in a duel aboard the Star Forge]] Revan managed to stop the droids from attacking him and followed Malak to the observation tower. Malak realized that his former Master's powers had surpassed those that he had held during his reign as the Dark Lord. Malak proclaimed his intention to break Revan's will and make his former Master his apprentice, as Revan would be a far greater asset than Shan and her battle meditation. However, Malak feared that Revan was too powerful to be his apprentice, believing that Revan would betray him should the former Sith Lord become the stronger of the two. After Revan retorted that he would not turn to the dark side again, the two began a lightsaber duel, in which the victor, unlike in their previous duels, would decide the fate of the galaxy. Malak and Revan fought a fierce duel in the observation tower. However, Malak withdrew from battle, explaining to his former Master that he could not be defeated. He then told Revan that he had brought the bodies of eight Jedi from the Jedi Enclave on Dantooine to the Star Forge and explained that rather than letting the deceased Jedi become one with the Force, he had used the Star Forge to corrupt what remained of the dead Jedi's Force energy. Malak used the Force to [[Force drain/Legends|drain]] the life force from one of the Jedi, transferring it to himself. After replenishing his vitality, Malak resumed his duel with Revan. However, the former Sith Lord freed as many of the captive Jedi as he could, allowing them to become one with the Force and shortening Malak's supply of energy. Malak was eventually brought to his knees, mortally wounded. He began to regret turning to the dark side, and Revan apologized to his old friend for starting him down the dark path. Malak was reminded by his former Master that he had chosen to continue to practice the dark side, and Malak told Revan that perhaps there was more truth in the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] than he had ever believed. The dying Dark Lord realized that he alone had to accept responsibility for his fate. With his dying words, Malak recognized that as the darkness took him, he was nothing in the end. Malak died with sorrow and regret over his deeds, but without hatred or malice toward Revan, his one-time closest friend. Malak's corpse was consumed by the flames of the failing Star Forge as the Republic fleet destroyed the ancient space station. ===Legacy=== ====Fate of the galaxy==== {{Quote|Revan and Malak did not fall to the dark side in a single moment. They turned after years spent in war and in defiance of the Jedi Council. Revan and Malak's descent into darkness actually began with compassion—the compassion that compelled them to enter the Mandalorian Wars.|Jedi Master Gnost-Dural|audio=GnostRevanMalakFall.flac|{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 9: The Mandalorian Wars|int=Timeline 9: The Mandalorian Wars}}}} [[File:DarthMalak-Timeline1.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Darth Malak's image was featured in a record by Jedi Master Gnost-Dural three centuries after the Sith Lord's death.]] [[3954 BBY|Two years]] after Darth Malak's death and the defeat of his Sith Empire, Revan started to recover his memories from his time as the Dark Lord and vanished from known Republic space to search for and defeat the Sith Emperor and his Sith Empire. Although Malak had died in his duel with Revan, the Dark Lord's final confrontation did not mark the last anyone saw of him. [[3951 BBY|Three years]] after Revan's disappearance, Meetra Surik, who served as a Jedi General under Malak and Revan during the Mandalorian Wars, discovered the [[Tomb of Ludo Kressh|lost tomb]] of the ancient Sith Lord [[Ludo Kressh]] while exploring a [[shyrack cave]] on Korriban. Surik encountered visions from her past, the first of which was an apparition of the young Malak before his fall to the dark side. As Surik looked on, Malak slowly lured several Jedi to his side. When Surik refused to join him, Malak and the other apparitions attacked; however, Surik prevailed over the visions. Shortly after this, Surik defeated the [[Sith Triumvirate]] and their Sith forces, which were comprised of forces that served under Malak's Sith Empire. Revan never returned from the Unknown Regions, as he was captured by the true Sith Empire, and [[3681 BBY|three centuries]] after Malak's death, the Sith Empire launched an attack on the Republic. This ignited a war that lasted for decades until the Sith Emperor signed a [[Treaty of Coruscant|treaty]] with the Republic.''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' Images of Malak were featured in four [[Holodocumentary|holographic records]] produced by the [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] [[Jedi archivist/Legends|Jedi archivist]] [[Gnost-Dural]]—who investigated Malak and Revan's significance to the Sith Emperor whose forces had besieged the galaxy in Gnost-Dural's time—for the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]].{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant|int=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant}}{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War|int=Timeline 10: The Exar Kun War}} In addition, a collection of Darth Malak's writings, known as the [[Morrigan Scrolls]], survived for some time after his death. Containing a wealth of Sith knowledge and tactics from the Jedi Civil War, the Morrigan Scrolls were worth thousands of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to collectors who were aware of the work's significance.''[[Lords of Nal Hutta]]'' ====Others' thoughts and historical research==== {{Quote|Unlike Revan, Malak demonstrated no concern for the future of the Republic in his attacks. His stratagems were painfully obvious, intending to crush all resistance, everywhere. There was little thought beyond the complete destruction of anything that opposed him. He left quite a mess. I'm still trying to assess all the damage. Between the two, I would have preferred Revan rule the galaxy. He had foresight in his conquest, a subtlety that Malak did not possess.|G0-T0, to Meetra Surik|audio=G0T0Malak.flac|}} [[File:Gotofull.jpg|thumb|left|200px|G0-T0 saw Malak as a messy brute.]] The atrocities that Darth Malak committed overshadowed his good deeds as a Jedi Knight in galactic history. While Revan was remembered and admired by many for his tactical genius and subtleties, and was celebrated as a hero both before and after his corruption for winning the Mandalorian Wars and Jedi Civil War, Malak by contrast was remembered by others, such as [[Exchange]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]] [[G0-T0]], as something of a brute who had left the galaxy in a mess. Many [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] thought the situation would have been different if Revan had remained the Dark Lord of the Sith, believing that Revan's strategies would have led to a stronger and more unified galaxy, while Malak's genocidal reign had only caused a greater destabilization of the galaxy and nearly destroyed all of his former Master's original goals. In contrast to Revan, Malak used brute force and excessive violence rather than strategy to defeat his enemies, as demonstrated when he ordered the bombing of Telos IV, the destruction of Taris, and finally the bombing of Dantooine The destruction that Malak caused exposed him as a monster who, despite all his attempts to gain absolute power, was still inferior compared to Revan. Malak was documented in historical texts of the Jedi Order such as Gnost-Dural's [[The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural|journal of his historical research]],[[The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural (real-life book)|''The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural'']] completed in [[3651 BBY]],Chapter 10—the last entry of [[The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural (real-life book)|''The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural'']]—is dated to two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Treaty of Coruscant]] which happened in [[3653 BBY]] according to the ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''. Therefore, the journal was completed in [[3651 BBY]]. ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'',[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] first released approximately in or after [[990 BBY]],[[Jedi historian]]s of the [[New Jedi Order]] estimate that ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'' initially came out a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] or more after the end of the [[New Sith Wars]] in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].
